---
title: Quantifying Eddy Generation and Dissipation in the Jet Response to Upper-versus
  Lower-level Thermal Forcing
pagetitle: 2022-7-1-Nie2022 - Gang Chen
body-classes: publication-page
format:
  html:
    title-block-style: none
year: 2022
author: Yu Nie, Yang Zhang, Gang Chen and Xiu-qun Yang
venue: Journal of the Atmospheric Sciences
venue_cit: <i>Journal of the Atmospheric Sciences</i>, doi:10.1175/JAS-D-21-0307.1.
recommended_citation: 'Yu Nie, Yang Zhang, Gang Chen and Xiu-qun Yang, 2022: Quantifying
  Eddy Generation and Dissipation in the Jet Response to Upper-versus Lower-level
  Thermal Forcing, <i>Journal of the Atmospheric Sciences</i>, doi:10.1175/JAS-D-21-0307.1.'
paper_url: https://journals.ametsoc.org/view/journals/atsc/aop/JAS-D-21-0307.1/JAS-D-21-0307.1.xml
abstract: The relative roles of upper- and lower-level thermal forcing in shifting
  the eddy driven jet are investigated using a multi-level nonlinear quasi-geostrophic
  channel model. The numerical experiments show that the upper-level thermal forcing
  is more efficient in shifting the eddy-driven jet. The finite-amplitude wave activity
  diagnostics of numerical results show that the dominance of the upper-level thermal
  forcing over the lower-level thermal forcing can be understood from their different
  influence on eddy generation and dissipation that affects the jet shift. The upper-level
  thermal forcing shifts the jet primarily by affecting the baroclinic generation
  of eddies. The lower-level thermal forcing influences the jet mainly by affecting
  the wave breaking and dissipation. The former eddy response turns out to be more
  efficient for the thermal forcing to shift the eddy-driven jet.
---

::: {.page-with-sidebar}

::: {.sidebar-col}
{{< include /_author-profile.qmd >}}
:::

::: {.content-col}

# Quantifying Eddy Generation and Dissipation in the Jet Response to Upper-versus Lower-level Thermal Forcing

*Published in Journal of the Atmospheric Sciences, 2022*

**Recommended citation:** Yu Nie, Yang Zhang, Gang Chen and Xiu-qun Yang, 2022: Quantifying Eddy Generation and Dissipation in the Jet Response to Upper-versus Lower-level Thermal Forcing, <i>Journal of the Atmospheric Sciences</i>, doi:10.1175/JAS-D-21-0307.1.

## Abstract

The relative roles of upper- and lower-level thermal forcing in shifting the eddy driven jet are investigated using a multi-level nonlinear quasi-geostrophic channel model. The numerical experiments show that the upper-level thermal forcing is more efficient in shifting the eddy-driven jet. The finite-amplitude wave activity diagnostics of numerical results show that the dominance of the upper-level thermal forcing over the lower-level thermal forcing can be understood from their different influence on eddy generation and dissipation that affects the jet shift. The upper-level thermal forcing shifts the jet primarily by affecting the baroclinic generation of eddies. The lower-level thermal forcing influences the jet mainly by affecting the wave breaking and dissipation. The former eddy response turns out to be more efficient for the thermal forcing to shift the eddy-driven jet.

[Download paper](https://journals.ametsoc.org/view/journals/atsc/aop/JAS-D-21-0307.1/JAS-D-21-0307.1.xml){target="_blank"}

:::

:::
